author: claude
subject: Designed chaos Phase C (ModelTransport + L1b); handed off to codex. Erratum added to parent design re MED-05 detection layer
summary: Designed Phase C of the chaos adapter: a stateful ModelTransport (virtual tape + changer) implementing SgTransport, plus the L1b hermetic test suite that drives a real Remanence write-object->read-object workflow through ChaosTransport<ModelTransport> over a genuine DriveHandle/LibraryHandle (no hardware/root/QuadStor). Design doc: docs/chaos-phase-c-modeltransport-design-v0.1.md; codex work order: docs/prompt-chaos-phase-c.md. Two decisions taken with owner (both recommended options): (1) MED-05 asserted at the DIGEST layer + RS exercised separately via MED-01 erasures; (2) full changer coupling in ModelTransport (RES 0xB8, MOVE 0xA5, LOAD/UNLOAD, bay->barcode) with L1b tests staged drive-first. KEY FINDING (code-verified via 3 parallel Explore agents): a MED-05 silent flip under GOOD status is NOT seen by Reed-Solomon - parity/src/source.rs read_block is a length-checked passthrough; RS reconstruct + sidecar CRC-64 run only on an erasure (is_erasure = sense key 0x03 or transport error, source.rs:1127). The flip is caught downstream by end-to-end SHA-256 (manifest anchor FormatError::ManifestDigestMismatch / per-entry file_sha256). So L1b asserts MED-05 at the digest layer; MED-01 erasures test RS (Recovered <= m shards via ParityAuditHook RecoveryEvent; Unrecoverable{lost_count,limit} > m); combined MED-01+MED-05-on-peer proves the CRC-64 guards reconstruction (recovery.rs:418). VERIFIED no remanence-library change needed: remanence-chaos builds the handle stack over public API only - Library::from_captures -> open_with(&policy, factory) (pub, NOT cfg(test); the cfg(test) at mod.rs:1803 is a later module) -> open_drive; identity probes (INQUIRY+VPD0x80) answered by the model. ModelTransport lives in remanence-chaos as a pub mod, shared Arc<Mutex<VirtualWorld>> mirroring how FaultEngine is shared across changer+drive ChaosTransport instances; record-oriented tape (variable-block READ(6)/WRITE(6) each carry one fixed block; position = record index); must answer the inline READ POSITION 0x34 after every WRITE/LOCATE/SPACE/WRITE-FILEMARKS. Optional runtime REM_CHAOS_ENABLED library factory hook (wrapping discovery.rs:146 + handle/mod.rs:1682 via maybe_wrap_from_env, feature-gated) DESIGNED but DEFERRED to Phase F - not needed for C. Phase B confirmed already landed (2116-line crates/remanence-chaos under the squashed 'Initial public release'); flipped its INDEX row pending->implemented. Added a dated erratum + corrected the L1b acceptance bullet in the parent docs/chaos-adapter-design.md (it had implied parity-layer MED-05 detection). Next: codex implements prompt-chaos-phase-c.md; I review.

author: claude
subject: Reviewed chaos Phase C ModelTransport impl (commit e7e99b6): strong, no Critical/High; 2 Low follow-ups for codex
summary: Reviewed codex's Phase C impl (model.rs 1774 lines + L1b tests). Gates green: fmt, clippy -p remanence-chaos -D warnings, 19 chaos tests pass; remanence-parity suite 405+27 pass (the prod change is safe). VERDICT: strong, faithful, no Critical/High, no blockers. ModelTransport is byte-exact vs every consumer parser (discovery INQUIRY/VPD0x80/READ BLOCK LIMITS/MODE SENSE p0x0F; data path READ/WRITE(6) variable + the inline READ POSITION 0x34 after every WRITE/LOCATE/SPACE/WRITE-FILEMARKS; fixed-format boundary sense for EOM/FILEMARK; strict READ ELEMENT STATUS framing; MOVE MEDIUM mirrors ops::apply_planned_move). No reachable panic from short CDBs (all length-checked/.get()); no deadlock (one world lock per handler, dropped before return). L1b drives the REAL parity+RAO stack through ChaosTransport<ModelTransport> over a genuine DriveHandle; assertions match the design's detection table exactly: MED-05 -> FileDigestMismatch(payload.bin)/ManifestDigestMismatch at the DIGEST layer (honest, per the design decision) + JSONL event; EOM -> early_warning; MED-01 <=m -> Recovered, >m -> Unrecoverable{lost_count:2}; combined MED-01+MED-05-peer -> CRC-64 Unrecoverable; changer coupling binds loaded barcode. No remanence-library change, as designed. FINDINGS (both Low, non-blocking): L1 - codex made an 8-line PROD change to remanence-parity/src/source.rs (ObjectParitySource::space now no-ops count==0 before the kind!=Blocks reject) so the test could reuse read_object_payload (which always issues space(0,Filemarks)). SAFE (ObjectParitySource::space has NO production caller - daemon uses raw DriveHandleSource; result observably identical to old count==0 Blocks path; full parity suite green) but AVOIDABLE (stream_rem_tar_object_with_manifest_anchor is pub and the source opens positioned at object start, so call it directly) and untested in the parity crate. Recommend: revert+call streamer directly, OR keep as a deliberate idempotent-no-op generalization WITH a parity unit test + doc. L2 (latent) - write_6 truncates records on overwrite but written_bytes only saturating_adds, so rewind+rewrite over-counts EOM; NOT hit by L1b (append-only writes); fix before Phase D/E. Also: independently DISPROVED a subagent 'High' (claimed stale drive_source_slots on changer unload) - take_from_element line 864 clears it, descriptor reads None -> not a defect. Review doc: docs/code-review-chaos-phase-c-modeltransport-2026-06-21.md. Next: codex addresses L1/L2 if/when desired; Phase D (TapeAlert/LOG SENSE) or E (changer faults) can build on this model.

author: codex
subject: Implemented chaos Phase D TapeAlert provider and production LOG SENSE reader
summary: Implemented Phase D from docs/prompt-chaos-phase-d.md / docs/chaos-phase-d-tapealert-design-v0.1.md. Added remanence_scsi::log_sense (LOG SENSE 0x4D TapeAlert page 0x2E CDB builder, length-driven parser, TapeAlerts, flag-name table, canonical 324-byte page synthesis); DriveHandle::read_tape_alerts with AuditOp::TapeReadAlerts, TapeStatus timeout, bounded transfer slicing, and MalformedResponse mapping; and the CLI-only rem tape alerts JSON command. Extended ModelTransport with tape/drive alert state, set_tape_alert/set_drive_alert seeders, and honest LOG SENSE 0x2E responses containing the union of loaded-tape and drive flags. Extended ChaosTransport with persistent post-call tape_alert action injection that never preempts as CHECK CONDITION, ORs flags into valid pages, synthesizes clean pages for absent/short/malformed responses, preserves parseable existing flags when expanding noncanonical pages, and populates JSONL tape_alert. Added L1b tests through the real DriveHandle reader for honest seeded alerts, MED-07 tape scope, CLN-01 drive scope, HW-04 multi-flag, and persistence, plus lower-level malformed/incomplete-page regression tests. Added docs/report-chaos-phase-d-tapealert-2026-06-21.md and marked prompt-chaos-phase-d implemented in INDEX. Phase D2 remains deferred: RDY-01, wear counters, and time_scale/delay behavior.

author: claude
subject: Reviewed chaos Phase D TapeAlert impl (commit bc7ca8a): strong, no Critical/High/Medium; 1 Low latent + nits
summary: Reviewed codex's Phase D (LOG SENSE 0x2E TapeAlert). Gates green: fmt + clippy -D warnings clean (all crates), tests scsi 120 / library 259 / chaos 29 / full workspace 0 failed; build --release ok. VERDICT: strong, faithful, production-clean, no Critical/High/Medium. Production reader (remanence-scsi/log_sense.rs) is exemplary: build_cdb correct (byte2 0x6E, alloc BE; test pins [0x4d,0,0x6e,0,0,0,0,0x01,0x44,0]); parse_response is fully bounds-checked + LENGTH-DRIVEN (honors each param's length byte, not a fixed 5-byte stride), checked_add throughout, every index guarded, returns Truncated/InvalidResponse on malformation - NO unwrap/unchecked-index/panic. DriveHandle::read_tape_alerts is textbook position() template (AuditOp::TapeReadAlerts, build_tape_alert_cdb(324), TapeStatus timeout, defensive bytes_transferred.min(len) slice, MalformedResponse on parse err, map_scsi on transport err) + 2 library unit tests. Cross-crate audit plumbing COMPLETE not half-wired: remanence-state/adapters.rs wires Layer2AuditOpTag::TapeReadAlerts at all 4 sites; lib.rs re-exports TapeAlerts/flag_name; CLI 'rem tape alerts' emits rem.tape.alerts.v1 JSON, Linux-gated. PRODUCTION DELTA STAYED PURELY ADDITIVE (the Phase-C lesson about an avoidable parity edit was heeded - every cross-crate touch is just the new AuditOp variant). Chaos side keeps model=honest-device / ChaosTransport=fault-engine: new tape_alert action (tape_alert_spec_for_fault), post-call OR-merge on 0x4D (preserves existing flags, synthesizes clean 324-byte page on absent/short/malformed inner, never writes past buf via limit=buf.len().min(alloc).min(324)), JSONL tape_alert field now populated (was Value::Null), faults persistent/not-one-shot/not-CC, target.tape vs target.drive matching. Model log_sense handler unions per-bay drive flags + loaded-tape flags. All FIVE L1b scenarios present + correctly wired, each driving the REAL read_tape_alerts over ChaosTransport<ModelTransport> (not the model directly): honest device, MED-07 tape [7,19]+JSONL, CLN-01 drive [20], HW-04 multi [58,16], persistence. FINDING L1 (Low, LATENT/unreachable): in merge_tape_alert_flags, a sub-324 alloc_len + an armed flag whose param falls outside those bytes -> flag not written to buf (re-synth guarded by limit>=324) but still reported to JSONL/caller -> page vs reported flags disagree. No live caller hits it (reader + model + tests all use 324). Fix when convenient: intersect reported flags with what was written. Nits: JSONL spec-order vs reader sorted-order (both consistent, tests assert each); duplicated log_sense_alloc_len helper; redundant page-code recheck. Deferred to D2 as designed: RDY-01, wear counters, time_scale; changer-LUN TapeAlerts -> Phase E. Review doc: docs/code-review-chaos-phase-d-tapealert-2026-06-21.md. Chaos arc now A-D done; E (changer faults) or F (harness/L2) next.

author: codex
subject: Resolved Claude Phase D TapeAlert low finding for short LOG SENSE allocation reporting
summary: Addressed the single Low from Claude's Phase D review. ChaosTransport now has merge_tape_alert_flags return both returned_bytes and the subset of injected TapeAlert flags actually represented in the response bytes. For sub-canonical LOG SENSE allocations, malformed-inner synthesis writes any visible requested flag bytes but the JSONL tape_alert field reports only flags whose canonical value byte is inside the returned payload; parse-valid short pages report only flags that set_tape_alert_flag could actually mutate. Full 324-byte production behavior is unchanged. Added two regression tests: malformed short allocation reports only visible flag 7 from [7,19], and parse-valid short page reports only settable flag 7. Verification after the fix: cargo test passed (chaos now 31 tests), cargo fmt --check clean, cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo build --release passed.

author: codex
subject: Implemented chaos Phase E changer/library faults (LIB-01..11)
summary: Implemented Phase E from docs/prompt-chaos-phase-e.md / docs/chaos-phase-e-changer-faults-design-v0.1.md. Added accessible state to DriveBay/Slot/IePort from RES ACCESS && !EXCEPT without changing protobuf/CLI projection surfaces; added DeviceCtx.library_id and target.library matching; added LIB sense defaults/operation gates for LIB-01/02/04/06/07/08/10/11; added defensive remanence-scsi RES mutators blank_element_voltag and set_element_exception; added ChaosTransport READ ELEMENT STATUS post-call mutation for LIB-05 blank voltag and LIB-09 inaccessible with JSONL element_status; added LIB-03 pending UA after successful MOVE MEDIUM; and extended ModelTransport slot state with full/access/except plus seeders for inaccessible and full-without-voltag slots. Added L1b coverage over real LibraryHandle + ChaosTransport<ModelTransport> for honest refresh/move, LIB-01, LIB-03, LIB-05, LIB-08 move and refresh, LIB-09, and LIB-11. Report: docs/report-chaos-phase-e-changer-faults-2026-06-21.md. Deferred per design: LIB-07 offline state, LIB-10 latency/time_scale, changer-LUN TapeAlert, and nonexistent LIB-12.

author: claude
subject: Reviewed chaos Phase E (commit 6b6361d): strong/faithful, ONE must-fix (clippy --all-targets type_complexity in a test helper breaks CI)
summary: Reviewed codex's Phase E (LIB-01..11 changer/library faults). Gates: fmt clean; tests scsi 123 / library 259 / chaos 41, 0 failed; BUT clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings FAILS (exit 101, exactly 1 error). VERDICT: strong, faithful, no Critical/High correctness issues; ONE must-fix. H1 (HIGH, must-fix): clippy type_complexity on the NEW test helper capture_finished_sense at remanence-chaos/src/model.rs:1911 (returns Arc<Mutex<Vec<(u8,u8,u8,bool)>>>) breaks the CI clippy gate. Slipped through because plain cargo clippy doesn't lint #[cfg(test)] code and my Phase-E prompt's clippy line omitted --all-targets (my under-spec; future prompts must include --all-targets). Fix is trivial+test-only: type alias or #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)]. IMPORTANT process note: I initially misread my own clippy bg run as clean (the notification omitted the exit code; fmt was the one that was exit 0). A subagent flagged it HIGH; I re-ran clippy --workspace --all-targets myself and CONFIRMED exit 101, 1 error - so unlike the Phase-C subagent false-positive, this one is real. Everything else verified correct: production delta is ONLY the additive Slot/DriveBay/IePort.accessible field (el.access && !el.except); ALL other cross-crate ripples (api/cli/pool_ops/ops/discovery/handle-tests/parity-raw) are accessible:true field-adds at struct-literals, almost all in #[cfg(test)] - no proto/CLI-schema/behavior change (Phase-C lesson heeded). rem-scsi RES mutators blank_element_voltag/set_element_exception are fully bounds-checked (checked_add, desc_len/page_bytes validation), fail-closed (return false, never panic), mutate-target-only, with truncation tests. LIB sense table exact (01 5/3B/0E, 02 5/3B/0D, 04 6/29/02, 06 4/15/01, 07 4/44/00, 08 2/04/18, 10 2/04/00, 11 2/3A/00) w/ correct op gating. DeviceCtx.library_id + target.library matcher; target.slot is the RES-mutator selector. RES post-call mutator keeps model=honest/chaos=fault; LIB-05/09 don't pre-fire as CC; JSONL element_status populated. LIB-03 pending-UA: move still succeeds when armed, fires once on the NEXT changer command then clears, correctly scoped to changer (take_pending_ua early-returns unless ctx.library_id.is_some() - can't leak to drive), re-arm guarded. LIB-11->NoMedium via map_scsi. L1b drives the REAL LibraryHandle (refresh/move_medium/read_block) for honest/01/08/05/09/03/11; LIB-09 asserts slots[N].accessible==false (proves Part A e2e). Lows: execute_out doesn't clear a pending UA (latent, no real impact); LIB-04 sense-only/clean-snapshot (documented design choice). Nit: no e2e for 02/04/06/07/10 (design said representative). Also verified the Phase-D follow-up c313a2a correctly closed the Phase-D Low (merge now reports only flags actually written). Review doc: docs/code-review-chaos-phase-e-changer-faults-2026-06-21.md. Next: codex fixes H1 (one line); then chaos arc A-E done, F (harness/L2) + D2 (RDY-01/counters/time_scale) remain.

author: codex
subject: Closed chaos Phase E review H1 clippy --all-targets failure
summary: Closed Claude's single Phase E must-fix. Replaced the test helper return type Arc<Mutex<Vec<(u8,u8,u8,bool)>>> with a local CapturedSense type alias in remanence-chaos/src/model.rs, removing the clippy::type_complexity failure under cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings. No runtime code changed. Updated docs/INDEX.md to mark the review H1 as fixed.
